title: A3 — Doctor lint #26 `relevance_fields_missing` + --apply-lint mutation arm + aggregate migration event
description: Add doctor lint #26 `relevance_fields_missing` that scans .fabric/knowledge/pending/**/*.md for frontmatter missing `relevance_scope` or `relevance_paths`. Detect mode reports findings; --apply-lint mode writes `relevance_scope: broad` + `relevance_paths: []` (matching meta-builder default) and emits one aggregate `relevance_migration_run` event with touched_count. Idempotent: skip files with both fields already present.

files.target: Lint registry + new check function + --apply-lint mutation arm
files.change: Add LINT_26_RELEVANCE_FIELDS_MISSING; reuse RELEVANCE_SCOPE_LINE_PATTERN/RELEVANCE_PATHS_LINE_PATTERN (L627-628) to detect absence; mutation appends YAML lines verbatim matching the regex shape; emit aggregate event after run
Add relevanceMigrationRunEventSchema { event_type: 'relevance_migration_run', timestamp, touched_count: number, scanned_count: number }; register in eventLedgerEventSchema discriminated union

Add 3 test cases: (a) detect-mode reports missing fields, (b) apply-lint mutation writes correct YAML and skips already-correct entries, (c) emits one relevance_migration_run event with accurate count

implementation: Define LINT_26 constant + descriptor (id: 26, name: 'relevance_fields_missing', severity: 'info', scope: 'pending')
Scan .fabric/knowledge/pending/**/*.md; for each file parse frontmatter; if relevance_scope OR relevance_paths absent → record finding

In --apply-lint branch: read file, append `relevance_scope: broad\nrelevance_paths: []` to frontmatter (insert before closing `---`), atomic-write via atomicWriteText; skip entries already containing both fields
After full scan emit one relevance_migration_run event with { touched_count, scanned_count } via appendEventLedgerEvent
Define + register new event schema in event-ledger.ts; export from package barrel
Add test fixtures (pending entries missing zero/one/both fields) and assertions covering all branches

reference.pattern: Existing doctor lint #23/#24/#25 implementation + --apply-lint mutation pattern
reference.examples: Mirror lint #23 (narrow_no_paths) structure; reuse iterateRelevanceFrontmatter helper (L3286-3299) for scan; mutation arm uses atomicWriteText

criteria: Doctor `--lint` mode lists lint #26 findings for every pending entry missing either field
Doctor `--apply-lint` mode writes `relevance_scope: broad` + `relevance_paths: []` to missing entries; running twice produces zero diff (idempotent)
Written YAML lines match RELEVANCE_SCOPE_LINE_PATTERN + RELEVANCE_PATHS_LINE_PATTERN regexes verbatim
Exactly one relevance_migration_run event emitted per --apply-lint invocation with correct touched_count
doctor.test.ts has 3+ new test cases (detect, apply, idempotent re-apply); all green
schemas-roundtrip.test.ts passes with new event type

rationale.chosen_approach: Doctor lint #26 + --apply-lint mutation; reuses tested mutation machinery, integrates with maintenance_hint_days cadence
alternatives_considered: Standalone scripts/migrate-frontmatter.mjs — one-shot, but no event audit trail
Per-file event (relevance_fields_migrated) instead of aggregate — noisier ledger
Skip migration entirely — relies on meta-builder fallback indefinitely
decision_factors: Migration is hygiene not correctness (meta-builder already defaults)
rationale.tradeoffs: Discoverability: users must run `fab doctor --apply-lint` to migrate; tradeoff accepted because the fallback safety net at knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1007-1021 makes migration optional

manual_checks: Create a pending entry without relevance_scope; run `fab doctor --lint` → see finding
Run `fab doctor --apply-lint` → verify YAML lines added + one event in .fabric/events.jsonl
Run again → zero changes + no new event (or aggregate event with touched_count=0)
success_metrics: Idempotent re-apply (zero diff on second run)
